title: Native-categorical XGBoost specialist
summary: The data has high-cardinality raw categories (`Driver`, `Race`, interactions by year/compound), and current best code mostly compresses them into frequency features.
rationale: The data has high-cardinality raw categories (`Driver`, `Race`, interactions by year/compound), and current best code mostly compresses them into frequency features. XGBoost’s native categorical partitioning can learn grouped category splits that freq encoding cannot express cleanly.
implementation_hint: Train a lean XGBoost specialist with `enable_categorical=True` on raw categorical columns plus a compact numeric state set, using race-held-out folds only. Compare against the current freq-encoded baseline, then use OOF rank averaging if it is complementary rather than individually superior.
expected_effect: Orthogonal gain from better categorical partitioning, especially for race/driver/compound combinations underrepresented by manual combo features.
risk: Native categorical handling can still overfit if folds are not strict by race/year, and category consistency must be preserved.
